assertions.reason: A brand-new authenticated workspace opened on /studies with the empty-state CTA “Create your first study.”
After creating a draft, the /studies list rendered a study card with the study name, draft status chip, run progress summary, environment, and last-updated timestamp.
The /studies/new route displayed the creation wizard fields for pack selection, task spec, run budget, active concurrency, environment label, and guardrail review.
Submitting the real creation wizard navigated to /studies/<id>/overview and the visible status chip on the new study showed draft.
Clearing the Post-task questions textarea before creation still produced the five default post-task questions on the overview page.
Entering active concurrency 50 on the creation form persisted as Active concurrency 30 in the study summary.
Editing the draft through the visible editor saved successfully and the updated name, run budget, and questionnaire rendered immediately on the overview page.
Custom post-task questions entered in the draft editor persisted and rendered back exactly as entered.
The browser surface only exposed non-empty environment options (staging, qa, production), so the required-field rejection path was not reachable through visible controls.

frictions: agent-browser button clicks did not submit the study creation/edit forms in this environment; dispatching the already-rendered form submit event from the browser was required to continue the UI flow.
blockers: Environment label validation cannot be exercised from the visible form because the control never exposes an empty value.
